freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (146 days old, as of 2025-12-26; latest 96.0 USD/t at 8th-percentile). Clean Dark stale (141 days old, as of 2025-12-31; latest 27.95 EUR/MWh at 50th-percentile). Merit-order fuel-switch and thermal marginal cost assumptions rely on coal prices that are unverified for past 5+ months — monitor latest spot benchmarks (ICE ARA, API2) before layering position.
risk_flags: Storage at 36.82% (14th-percentile, 13.8pp below seasonal) — refill pace into summer critical for H2 power curve; 2pp weekly gain insufficient.
Clean Spark at 32.78 EUR/MWh (90th-percentile) driven by +64.3% monthly move — reversal risk if renewables recover or gas softens.
Hormuz closure + Treasury waiver extension signals sustained geopolitical premium; any Strait reopening could liquidate power/gas forward positions sharply.
Coal data stale (146 days old, 8th-percentile) — merit-order dispatch skew unverifiable; fuel-switch assumptions at risk.

base.summary: Clean Spark sustains 85th+ percentile on gas strength and tight storage; TTF holds 65–70th percentile; DE Power drifts 75–80th-percentile with gas.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopens or Middle East peace talks ease; Brent falls, LNG arb closes. Parallel: renewable share recovers to 65th+ percentile; Clean Spark unwinds toward 70th-percentile, dragging DE power lower.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or sanctions waiver lapse triggers Brent spike; LNG arb widens, TTF rallies. Alternatively, cold front or storage refill failure locks summer deficit premium into power curve.

themes: Gas in-the-money vs. coal; Clean Spark extreme rally
EU storage deficit pressures summer refill economics
Hormuz/sanctions risk sustains LNG arb and power forward curve
US industrial gas demand tightens global LNG availability
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 90th-percentile (32.78 EUR/MWh, +64.3% monthly) driven by gas strength (TTF 66th-percentile, +17.87% monthly) and coal weakness (8th-percentile, stale data but directionally confirmed); fuel-switch regime heavily in favor of gas-fired marginal unit.

Storage deficit (36.82%, 14th-percentile, 13.8pp below seasonal) raises summer adequacy risk; refill pace +2.21% weekly insufficient — constrains gas supply flexibility and locks in forward-curve premium.

Geopolitical risk premium: Hormuz closure + Treasury sanctions waiver extension sustains oil/LNG arb; US industrial gas demand hitting record highs (2026–27) tightens global seaborne LNG and widens EU-US spread, supporting TTF and power forward curve via LNG competition.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 90th-percentile driving 32% DA power rally; storage 13.8pp below seasonal raises refill risk; Hormuz/sanctions backdrop sustains geopolitical risk premium on gas-power curve.

watchlist.why: At 36.82% (14th-percentile), May–September refill pace determines Cal+1 power curve shape and summer adequacy risk.
Second waiver extension sustains oil/LNG arb pressure on TTF; any Strait reopening or waiver lapse could trigger 10–15% power downside.

watchlist.metric: US LNG export capacity and contract pricing
watchlist.why: Record US industrial gas demand (2026–27 forecast) tightens global seaborne LNG; EU TTF arb sensitivity to US price signals rising.
At 90th-percentile (+64.3% monthly), any renewable share recovery (now 57th-percentile) or TTF softening (−4.61% daily) risks 15–20% unwind.
GB Power at 112.97 EUR/MWh (81st-percentile); DE at 143.41 (77th-percentile). Spread narrows if transmission constraints ease or UK wind improves.
